notes.total: 264 tickets is a healthy quarter pipeline, reflecting strong capacity planning and a well-scoped backlog.
notes.completed: 244 of 264 tickets completed (92%) demonstrates excellent delivery performance and effective sprint execution.
notes.completion_rate: 92% completion rate is strong, with the remaining 16 in-progress items representing normal end-of-quarter tail work.
notes.releases_shipped: 30 releases across 6 sprints shows excellent shipping cadence, though the last release on 2026-05-21 occurred well after quarter end, indicating post-quarter cleanup or release process delays.
notes.oos_total: 35 out-of-sprint additions (13% of total) indicates disciplined sprint planning with minimal reactive work introduced mid-sprint.
notes.oos_open: All out-of-sprint items resolved by quarter end, demonstrating strong follow-through on unplanned work.
notes.type_split: Healthy balance across stories (37%), tasks (42%), and bugs (22%), with bug ratio well within acceptable range and no quality concerns.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 3 releases per sprint (mean 5.0) indicates consistent shipping rhythm, with some sprints batching multiple releases.
notes.time_logged: 3,706 hours logged against 4,746 estimated represents 78% utilization, suggesting either under-logging or conservative estimates; investigate time tracking practices.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 78% accuracy with 22% under-spend shows estimates are conservative but reasonable; team consistently delivers within or under estimated effort.
notes.no_estimate: 18 tickets (7%) completed without estimates is excellent hygiene, minimal impact on planning reliability.
notes.rollover: 24 tickets (9%) rolled over between sprints is low and acceptable, indicating sprints are generally well-scoped with minimal carryover.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 13 days is acceptable for complex work, though mean of 23 days indicates some tickets experience significant delays; investigate outliers blocking flow.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload well-distributed across team members, with Sandor Kocsis carrying the highest load at 50 tickets (19%) and all core team members maintaining 83-96% completion rates; no red flags on balance or individual performance.
